seo.title: Developer Tools implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Developer Tools projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for launching production-grade developer tools, ensuring reliability across environments, consistent UX, and stable distribution channels.

items.title: Standard Streams Separation
items.description: Direct all functional output to stdout and all logging, warnings, or error messages to stderr to ensure pipe compatibility.

items.title: Exit Code Standardization
items.description: Implement specific non-zero exit codes for different failure categories (e.g., 1 for general errors, 64 for usage errors) to support CI/CD scripting.

items.title: TTY Detection
items.description: Detect if the output is a TTY and automatically disable ANSI colors and interactive prompts when running in non-interactive environments like GitHub Actions.

items.title: Global Help and Version Flags
items.description: Verify that --help, -h, --version, and -v flags are responsive at the root and for every sub-command.

items.title: Progress Indicators
items.description: Use non-blocking progress bars or spinners for long-running tasks, ensuring they are suppressed in non-TTY environments to prevent log bloating.

items.title: Type Definition Shipments
items.description: Include native TypeScript declarations (.d.ts) or Rust/Go source types; ensure no 'any' types exist in public-facing interfaces.

items.title: Idempotency Implementation
items.description: Ensure all write operations in the SDK support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate resource creation during network retries.

items.title: Exponential Backoff
items.description: Hardcode an exponential backoff strategy for 429 (Rate Limit) and 5xx (Server Error) responses within the SDK core.

items.title: Custom Error Classes
items.description: Export named error classes (e.g., UnauthorizedError, ValidationError) so consumers can use 'instanceof' checks rather than string parsing.

items.title: Timeout Defaults
items.description: Set a global default timeout (e.g., 30 seconds) for all network requests to prevent hanging processes in user applications.

items.title: Cross-Platform Binary Compilation
items.description: Build and test binaries for x64 and ARM64 architectures across Linux, macOS, and Windows using a matrix CI build.

items.title: Checksum Verification
items.description: Generate and publish SHA-256 checksums for all downloadable assets to allow users to verify file integrity.

items.title: Lockfile Commitment
items.description: Ensure the lockfile (package-lock.json, Cargo.lock, etc.) is committed to the repository to guarantee reproducible builds.

items.title: Registry Metadata Audit
items.description: Populate 'repository', 'bugs', 'engines', and 'homepage' fields in the manifest file to ensure correct display on registries like npm or Crates.io.

items.title: Dependency Minimization
items.description: Run a dependency tree audit to remove unused packages and verify that all production dependencies are strictly necessary to minimize install size.

items.title: Secure Credential Storage
items.description: Store API tokens and secrets using system-level secret stores (e.g., macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Locker) instead of plain-text config files.

items.title: Environment Variable Precedence
items.description: Implement a configuration hierarchy where CLI flags override environment variables, and environment variables override local config files.

items.title: PII Scrubbing in Logs
items.description: Implement regex filters to automatically redact authorization headers, tokens, and local file paths from debug logs before they are written to disk.

items.title: Vulnerability Scanning
items.description: Integrate automated security scanning (e.g., Snyk, GitHub Dependabot) into the CI pipeline to block releases with known vulnerabilities.

items.title: Minimum Permission Scoping
items.description: Verify that the tool only requests the specific OAuth scopes or filesystem permissions required for its documented functionality.

items.title: Global Opt-out Mechanism
items.description: Support a 'DO_NOT_TRACK' environment variable and a config flag to completely disable all telemetry collection.

items.title: Anonymized Hardware Reporting
items.description: Ensure telemetry only collects non-identifiable data such as OS type, CPU architecture, and tool version.

items.title: Verbose Debug Mode
items.description: Implement a --debug or --verbose flag that provides a detailed execution trace for local troubleshooting without sending data to servers.

items.title: First-run Notice
items.description: Display a one-time clear notice on the first execution explaining what data is collected and how to opt-out.

items.title: Crash Reporting
items.description: Implement an automated crash reporting system that prompts the user for consent before uploading stack traces.

items.title: Executable Code Examples
items.description: Verify that every code snippet in the README or /examples directory compiles and runs against the current version.

items.title: Shell Autocomplete Scripts
items.description: Generate and distribute completion scripts for Bash, Zsh, and Fish shells.

items.title: Changelog Maintenance
items.description: Maintain a 'CHANGELOG.md' following 'Keep a Changelog' principles, categorized by Added, Changed, and Fixed.

items.title: Troubleshooting Reference
items.description: Include a dedicated documentation section mapping common error messages to specific resolution steps.

items.title: Inline Documentation
items.description: Ensure every CLI command and SDK method has JSDoc, Rustdoc, or GoDoc comments for IDE intellisense support.
